description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-data/dbt-image:v0.1.2 Docker image (and registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-data/data-image:data-image:v2.0.7 for the ds_exposure_dependencies_query job) with a shared before_script that exports Snowflake environment variables, loads an SSH key from $GIT_DATA_TESTS_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, sets CI_PROFILE_TARGET and DBT_RUNNER, and a shared after_script that copies the compiled dbt target to public/dbt; all jobs publish the public directory and transform/snowflake-dbt/logs as artifacts retained for one week. Define hidden template jobs .dbt_macro_check, .dbt_misc_jobs, .snowflake_dbt_jobs, .glab_job and .notification_job that use the same image, the analytics tag, manual or merge‑request only triggers, and inherit the common before/after scripts. Add concrete jobs clear_report and post_report_on_build extending .notification_job, which run only when merge‑request labels include or exclude "Suppress Results Report" and execute scripts to clear or post DBT result reports. Add run_grants, "🌱manual_seed", "🌻freshness", "🔛custom_invocation", "🏗️🏭build_changes", "💾data_tests", "📚✏️generate_dbt_docs", "📝specify_tests", "📈snapshots", and ds_exposure_dependencies_query jobs, each placed in the "🛠️ dbt Misc" or "⚙️ dbt Run" stage, running sequences such as dbt deps, dbt seed, dbt test, make run-grants, make build-changes, dbt docs generate, dbt source snapshot-freshness, or custom dbt statements, with manual when clauses and only rules that watch for changes to *.sql files or merge‑request events. Ensure every job uses the analytics tag, respects the defined rules, and uploads the compiled dbt files and logs as artifacts.
